1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).

content.content: 1 # BEHAVIOR.md — observed spec of the tamarin web-UI constraint-system graph payload 2 3 All statements below are derived from OBSERVED oracle output: the 81 captured 4 crawl manifests under `oracle/captured_responses/` (14705 graph payloads) and 5 targeted live probing of the black-box HS binary (see QUERIES.log). No 6 tamarin-prover source was read. Where a behavior could not be pinned down 7 black-box it is marked **[GAP]**. 8 9 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 10 ## 1. Transport / payload family 11 12 A crawl manifest is JSON: `{base, lemmas, log, capped, manifest}` where 13 `manifest` maps a URL to `{kind, status, body}` (kind ∈ html|text|json|dot). 14 15 Graph-bearing URL families (theory index `N` replaces `#` in captures): 16 17 | URL family | kind | payload | 18 |---|---|---| 19 | `/thy/trace/N/interactive-graph-def/proof/[/...]` | `dot` | **the graph** — graphviz DOT text (`text/plain`) | 20 | `/thy/trace/N/intdot/proof/[/...]` | `html` | wrapper page embedding `` | 21 | `/thy/trace/N/main/cases/{raw,refined}/i/j` | `json` | `{html,title}`; html contains `` | 22 23 The DOT text at `interactive-graph-def` is the whole deliverable. `intdot` is a 24 fixed HTML shell (only the `` and the `dotsrc` path vary). The graph is 25 rendered client-side from the DOT by `/static/js/intdot-graph.es.js`. 26 27 **JSON graph payload** [GAP]: the binary exposes a *separate* `--with-json` 28 rendering backend (`tamarin-prover interactive --help`). No JSON rendering tool 29 is installed here and no `interactive-graph-def` body in the corpus is JSON — 30 the entire corpus is the DOT backend. JSON graph format is therefore unobserved. 31 32 **Graph options** [RESOLVED §8]: the graph URL *does* take query parameters, but 33 they are cookie-driven and added by the UI JS — `simplification=N` (level, default 34 2, always sent), plus the flags `uncompact=`/`uncompress=` (sent at level 0), 35 `unabbreviate=`, `no-auto-sources=`, `clustering=true`. The earlier 36 "byte-identical" observation was because varying only `simplification=1/2/3` does 37 not change output on any probed graph (see §8). The real transforms are triggered 38 by the boolean flags. See §8. 39 40 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 41 ## 2. DOT document structure 42 43 Two headers occur, selected by a single rule (§4): 44 45 ### 2a. Simple header (non-clustered) 46 ``` 47 digraph "G" { 48 nodesep="0.3"; 49 ranksep="0.3"; 50 node[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica",width="0.3",height="0.2"]; 51 edge[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica"]; 52 <statements…> 53 54 } 55 ``` 56 57 ### 2b. Compact header (clustered) 58 ``` 59 digraph "G" { 60 nodesep="0.8"; 61 ranksep="0.8"; 62 sep="4"; 63 splines="true"; 64 overlap="false"; 65 pack="true"; 66 packmode="cluster"; 67 concentrate="true"; 68 compound="true"; 69 remincross="true"; 70 mclimit="10"; 71 nslimit="20"; 72 nslimit1="20"; 73 ordering="out"; 74 rankdir="TB"; 75 showboxes="false"; 76 clusterrank="local"; 77 node[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica",width="0.3",height="0.2",margin="0.05,0.05",shape="ellipse"]; 78 edge[fontsize="8",fontname="Helvetica",penwidth="1.5",arrowsize="0.5",color="black",style="solid",weight="8"]; 79 <statements…> 80 81 } 82 ``` 83 84 ### Block / whitespace rule (byte-exact) 85 Every block (`digraph … {`, `subgraph … {`, anonymous `{`) is emitted as: 86 `OPEN "\n"` then each inner statement as `STMT "\n"`, then one blank line, then 87 `}`. i.e. the body always ends `…\n\n}`. The top-level digraph is followed by a 88 final `\n`. Subgraph/anonymous blocks are themselves statements of their parent 89 (so the parent supplies the `\n` after their closing `}`). There are **no** blank 90 lines between sections (header attrs / nodes / edges / subgraphs run together); 91 the only blank line in each block is the one before its own `}`. 92 An empty graph is header attrs + blank + `}` (154 bytes). 93 94 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 95 ## 3. Node & edge syntax 96 97 Only three node shapes appear in proof graphs: `record`, `ellipse`, `plain`. 98 99 ### 3a. Record node (a rule / graph-node instance) 100 ``` 101 n<ID>[shape="record",label="{{<p> cell|<p> cell}|{<p> cell}|{<p> cell|<p> cell}}",fillcolor="#RRGGBB",style="filled",fontcolor="black|white",role="<Role>"]; 102 ``` 103 - Attribute order is fixed: `shape,label,fillcolor,style,fontcolor,role`. 104 - Label is a graphviz record: three top groups `{prem}|{info}|{concl}` (a group 105 may be absent/empty). Each group = cells joined by `|`. Each cell = 106 `<portid> text` (port id like `n0`, a space, then the fact text). Ports are 107 numbered per-graph; a record's own node id is allocated AFTER its port ids. 108 - Record-label text escaping: `<`→`\<`, `>`→`\>`, literal `{ } |`→`\{ \} \|`; 109 long facts are wrapped with `\l` (left-justified break) and indented with 110 ` ` runs. (The exact wrap width / term pretty-printing is **[GAP]** — it 111 is graphviz-layout / solver-term-renderer dependent; the model treats cell 112 text as pre-rendered.) 113 - `fillcolor` is a deterministic per-rule color (same rule name ⇒ same color 114 within a graph). `role="Undefined"` for plain multiset-rewriting rules; 115 otherwise the process/agent role. `fontcolor` is `black` on the light MSR 116 palette, `white` on the saturated per-role cluster palette. Exact color hash 117 is **[GAP]**; the model carries colors explicitly. 118 119 ### 3b. Ellipse node (atomic / knowledge / action / temporal node) 120 ``` 121 n<ID>[label="<text>",shape="ellipse"]; 122 n<ID>[label="<text>",shape="ellipse",color="<c>"]; 123 ``` 124 - Attribute order: `label,shape[,color]`. Optional `color` (`gray` for `!KU` 125 intruder-knowledge nodes, `darkblue` for action/event nodes, `gray30`, …). 126 - Ellipse text is NOT record-escaped (`<`,`>` appear literally, e.g. 127 `Session( 'I', <pk(~ltkA), pkB>, … )`). 128 129 ### 3c. Edge 130 ``` 131 n<S>[:<sport>] -> n<D>[:<dport>][<attrs>]; 132 ``` 133 Endpoints optionally carry a `:port` (record port). `<attrs>` is a 134 comma-separated attribute list, in observed key order. Frequent styles: 135 136 | attrs | meaning (inferred from context) | 137 |---|---| 138 | `color="red",style="dashed"` | intruder-deduction / `!KU` edge | 139 | `style="bold",weight="10.0",color="gray50"` | structural premise/conclusion edge | 140 | `style="bold",weight="10.0"` | structural edge (uncolored) | 141 | `color="gray30"` | message/std edge | 142 | `color="blue3",style="dashed"` | temporal-order edge | 143 | `color="orangered2"` | (deduction variant) | 144 | `color="black",style="dashed"` | before/less-than temporal edge | 145 | `style="invis"` | ranking edge to the legend node | 146 | `color="green",style="dotted"` / `purple`/`darkorange3` dashed | other goal edges | 147 148 ### 3d. Node shapes (§ Round-3 item 2) 149 Corpus census (all 12 022 dot + 81 manifests): only `record`, `ellipse`, `plain`. 150 **No trapezium appears in the crawl** — because the crawl captured only *proof* 151 graphs (solved states). Live probing (NAXOS_eCK, NSLPK3 case-distinction graphs) 152 adds one more shape: 153 - **`invtrapezium`** — a `(#var, idx)` placeholder for an **unresolved graph node** 154 referenced by a still-open premise, e.g. `n9[label="(#i, 0)",shape="invtrapezium"]`. 155 `#var` is the (temporal) node variable, `idx` the premise index; the node is fed 156 by a structural edge from the conclusion that must satisfy that premise 157 (`n5:n2 -> n9[style="bold",weight="10.0",color="gray50"]`). Appears in both the 158 compressed default and the uncompressed variant, in every source-case graph 159 tested. Attr order = ellipse's (`label,shape`). 160 - **`trapezium`** (spec-named dual) was **NOT observed** in any probe (all case 161 graphs of two theories, compressed + uncompressed) nor the corpus. Recorded as 162 unobserved; the serializer accepts any shape string so it can be emitted if a 163 caller supplies one. 164 165 ### 3e. Node-id / port allocation (§ Round-3 item 3) — byte-verified over 12 022 166 One global monotonic counter, ids handed out in **emission order** so file order == 167 id order. For each node in order: 168 - a **record** takes one id per cell (in cell order: premises, then the info cell, 169 then conclusions) and **then** one id for the node itself — so a `k`-cell record 170 occupies `n<p>…n<p+k-1>` (ports) and `n<p+k>` (node); 171 - every other kind (ellipse / plain / invtrapezium) takes exactly one id. 172 173 Example: a 4-prem/1-info/3-concl record takes ports `n0…n6`, node `n7`; the next 174 ellipse is `n8`. Verified 12022/12022 in `mine_ids.py` and again in Rust 175 (`tests/alloc_corpus.rs`, via `NodeIdAllocator`). 176 177 ### 3f. Record-cell term rendering + line wrapping (§ Round-3 item 1) 178 **Group structure** (byte-verified over 160 409 records): the label is 179 `{grp}|{grp}|{grp}` where the groups are the *non-empty* ones among 180 `[premises, info, conclusions]`, in that order; the **info** group is always 181 present (100 %). An empty premise or conclusion group is dropped (a source rule 182 renders `{info}|{concl}`). 183 - **Info cell**: `#<temporal> : <RuleName>` and, if the rule has action facts, 184 `[<action>, …]` (e.g. `#i : I_Complete[Complete( … ), …]`, or bare `#vf.5 : Fresh`). 185 - **Premise/conclusion cell**: a single fact. 186 - **Fact spacing**: a fact pads — `Name( a, b )`, and `Name( )` with no args; 187 a **function application** does not — `f(a, b)`; a **tuple** is `<a, b>`. Verified 188 against the corpus. 189 - **Escaping**: inside a record label, `< > { } |` each get a leading backslash; 190 everything else (quotes, `~ $ ^ * ⊕`, spaces) is literal. 191 - **Line wrapping FORMAT** (byte-exact). When a group breaks, physical lines are 192 separated by `\l` (with a trailing `\l`), and each continuation line is indented 193 with a ` ` run **equal to the column of the broken group's first element** 194 (just after `( ` for a fact, after `<` for a tuple, after `[` for an action 195 list). Verified across 188 192 wrapped cells. 196 - **Line wrapping DECISION — RESOLVED (Session 4): a fixed-width paragraph fill, 197 width = 87 columns, measured per fact from column 0.** The Round-3 claim ("first 198 line 5..87, not a flat width, not derivable") is **refuted**: it *is* a flat 199 width. Established by driving crafted single-node theories 200 (`Out(<'a01', …, 'aN'>)`, `Out(<'aa…a', 'y'>)`) through the live server and 201 sweeping the term width one column at a time: 202 - **W = 87 absolute, functor-invariant.** A fact stays on one line iff its flat 203 rendering is ≤ 87 columns; at 88 it breaks. Tested with functor names of length 204 2, 3, 6, 10 (`Ba`,`Out`,`Fact12`,`Factabcdef`): the boundary was **always** at 205 flat width 87 fits / 88 breaks, so the budget is counted from the functor's own 206 column 0, *including* the functor name (a longer name simply fits fewer args). 207 Corpus-consistent: 99.2 % of 190 044 physical lines have fact-content ≤ 87; the 208 residual are single unbreakable atoms wider than 87 (which overflow verbatim). 209 - **Greedy paragraph fill.** When a group overflows, elements pack left-to-right; 210 the separator `, ` trails the element it follows and stays on the line, and the 211 next element wraps when it would pass column 87. Continuation lines use the 212 §3f indent. Example `Out(<'a01'…'a12'>)` (flat 91): line 0 = 213 `Out( <'a01', … 'a11', ` (eleven elements + trailing `, `, ending col 83), 214 `'a12'>` on the next line, then the fact's `)` on its own line. 215 - **Delimiter peel.** The fact's closing `)` peels onto its own physical line at 216 the **functor column** (col 0) whenever the fact breaks; a tuple's `>` peels to 217 the **`<` column** when the last element fills the line 218 (`Out( <'aa…(74)', 'y'` │ `>` │ `)`); an unbreakable atom wider than the budget 219 overflows and only the trailing delimiters wrap. 220 - **KNOWN RESIDUAL** (`fsep` lookahead): the underlying combinator's one-element 221 lookahead lets a *continuation* line hold **one more** element than the first 222 line at the same start column (first line 11, continuation 12 for the 5-col 223 `'aNN'` elements). The width (87), the top-level fit, the first-line packing and 224 the peel columns are byte-verified; reproducing the ±1 continuation lookahead 225 and the boundary peel byte-for-byte would require the exact `fsep`/`nest` doc 226 tree. `graph-clean::render` implements `FILL_WIDTH=87`, `fits_one_line`, and 227 `paragraph_fill` (first-line-exact), tested against these captured probes. 228 229 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 230 ## 4. Clustering / simplification (§ priority 3) 231 232 **Trigger rule (byte-verified over all 14705 graphs, 0 violations):** 233 > The COMPACT clustered rendering (§2b, with `subgraph "cluster_…"` blocks) is 234 > used **iff the graph contains at least one node with `role` ≠ `"Undefined"`.** 235 > Otherwise the SIMPLE header (§2a) is used. 236 237 Non-Undefined roles arise from process/SAPIC agents (e.g. `Initiator`, 238 `Responder`, `server`, `Tag`, `v`). Pure multiset-rewriting theories emit the 239 simple header everywhere; role-annotated theories emit the compact header on 240 every non-empty graph (empty graphs have no role nodes ⇒ simple). 241 242 ### Cluster block 243 ``` 244 subgraph "cluster_<Role>_Session_<k>" { 245 nodesep="0.6"; 246 ranksep="0.6"; 247 label="<Role>_Session_<k>"; 248 style="filled"; 249 color="#RRGGBBAA"; 250 penwidth="2"; 251 fillcolor="#RRGGBBAA"; 252 overlap="false"; 253 sep="4"; 254 n<ID>[shape="record",label="…",fillcolor="#RRGGBB",style="filled",fontcolor="white",role="<Role>"]; 255 256 } 257 ``` 258 - Cluster name = `cluster_<clusterlabel>`; `label` = the clusterlabel 259 (`<Role>_Session_<k>`). Nodes grouped by (role, session instance). 260 - `color`/`fillcolor` are an 8-hex ARGB (alpha `4C`, ~30%) hashed from the 261 cluster; node `fillcolor` is the saturated per-role color. Exact hash **[GAP]**. 262 263 ### What "simplification" means here 264 There is no exposed simplification *level*; the corpus reflects one fixed 265 rendering pipeline. The observable "simplification" is the fixed compression the 266 server applies before rendering: intruder-knowledge (`!KU`) nodes are drawn as 267 gray ellipses; single-premise/conclusion structural chains are drawn with bold 268 weight-10 edges; role nodes are packed into per-session clusters (compact mode). 269 Reproducing the *content* of that compression from a raw constraint system 270 requires the solver and is **[GAP]** — out of scope for a rendering crate. 271 272 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 273 ## 5. Node abbreviation (§ priority 2) 274 275 When a graph is drawn, sufficiently complex sub-terms are replaced by short 276 NAMES and listed in a legend node. Observed from 113 924 legend rows. 277 278 ### 5a. Legend node 279 A `plain` node with an HTML-like label, placed in a sink-rank block: 280 ``` 281 { 282 rank="sink"; 283 n<ID>[shape="plain",label=<<TABLE BORDER="1" CELLBORDER="0" CELLSPACING="3" CELLPADDING="1"><TR>…row0…</TR> 284 <65 spaces><TR>…row1…</TR> 285 … 286 <65 spaces><TR>…rowN…</TR></TABLE>>]; 287 288 } 289 ``` 290 - Each row: 291 `<TR><TD ALIGN="LEFT" VALIGN="TOP"><FONT COLOR="#000000">NAME</FONT></TD> <TD ALIGN="LEFT" VALIGN="TOP">=</TD> <TD ALIGN="LEFT" VALIGN="TOP">EXPANSION</TD></TR>` 292 (single spaces between `</TD>` and the next `<TD>`). 293 - The first `<TR>` is inline after the `<TABLE …>` tag; every continuation `<TR>` 294 is on its own line indented by **65 spaces = the byte length of the 295 `<TABLE BORDER="1" CELLBORDER="0" CELLSPACING="3" CELLPADDING="1">` opening 296 tag** (graphviz hang-indent of the table's children; independent of node id). 297 - EXPANSION is HTML-escaped: `<`→`<`, `>`→`>`, `&`→`&`. Single quotes 298 and `* ^ ~ $ ⊕` are literal. 299 - The legend node is wired to the rest with `style="invis"` edges (from a couple 300 of real nodes to the legend), emitted AFTER the sink block. 301 302 ### 5b. Naming scheme (byte-verified rule) 303 `NAME = <PREFIX><n>`, n = 1,2,3,… per prefix. 304 `PREFIX` = the first **two alphabetic characters, uppercased**, of the ROOT 305 symbol's *name* (one letter if the name has only one). The root symbol name is: 306 - function application `f(…)` ⇒ `f` (`sign`→SI, `senc`→SE, `hash`→HA, 307 `h`/`h1`/`h2`→H, `KDF`/`kdf1`→KD, `pk`→PK, `aenc`/`aead`→AE, `pmult`→PM, …) 308 - public constant `'c'` ⇒ `c` (`'uninitialized'`→UN, `'F_status'`→FS, 309 `'ho_req_ack'`→HO, `'no_message_state'`→NO — non-letters skipped: F,s→FS) 310 - variable `~v` / `$v` / `v` ⇒ `v` (`~AMF_UE_NGAP_ID`→AM, `~cid_N26`→CI, 311 `~gNB_UE_ID`→GN, `~mid…`→MI, `commitmsg`→CO, `StateChannel`→ST) 312 - infix operator, mapped to its function name then the letter rule: 313 `^`→exp→**EX**, `*`→mult→**MU**, `++`→union→**UN**, `⊕`→xor→**XO**. 314 315 Abbreviations NEST: an inner abbreviated term is referenced by name inside an 316 outer expansion (`KD1='KDF(EX1)'`, `MA1='mac(EX1, SI1)'`, `EX1="'g'^MU1"`, 317 `H1="h(<…, EX2, EX3, EX1, …>)"`). So naming is applied bottom-up. 318 319 Per-prefix numbering is by tamarin's canonical term order, NOT by first 320 appearance in the drawing (observed: `In(EX2)` is the first occurrence yet gets 321 `EX2`, and larger/deeper terms get the lower numbers within a prefix). The exact 322 tie-break total order is **[GAP]** (needs the solver's term ordering); the crate 323 assigns per-prefix numbers over a defined order and matches whenever that order 324 is unambiguous. 325 326 ### 5c. Selection — WHICH sub-terms get abbreviated **[RESOLVED — see REPORT2.md]** 327 328 **Rule (three necessary gates; each confirmed by a controlled black-box probe):** 329 > A sub-term `t` is abbreviated **iff** 330 > 1. `renderLen(t) ≥ 10` (Unicode-scalar length of the fully-expanded surface 331 > rendering, *including* `'…'` quotes and `~`/`$` sigils), AND 332 > 2. `occ(t) ≥ 2` (occurrences over the whole constraint system — graph node 333 > facts **and** the sequent's goals/formulas — edge-shared messages count 334 > once), AND 335 > 3. `t` is **not** a tuple `<…>`. 336 > Naming is bottom-up: nested eligible sub-terms are named first 337 > (`LO1='longarg123'`, then `H1='h(LO1)'`). 338 339 This is a rendered **length** gate, not the *size* or *occurrence* the earlier 340 note guessed at — which is why it looked context-free-yet-unexplained. It 341 cleanly settles the old counter-observations: `sign(<…,'g'^~ekR>,~ltkI)` is ≥10 342 chars (abbreviated); `KDF(z)` is 6 chars (never abbreviated, whatever its count); 343 `mac(z,SI1)` and `'g'^~lkR` are below the length/occurrence thresholds in their 344 graphs. `'g'^~lkR` vs `'g'^~lkR.1` differ because the `.1` copy reaches occ 2 in 345 the system while the other does not. 346 347 **Corpus-exactness (necessary direction):** across all **97 538** legend 348 expansions in **11 564** graphs, the minimum rendered length is exactly **10** 349 (0 below) and **0** entries are top-level tuples. Among terms drawn ≥2× in a 350 graph, **0** abbreviated terms violate len≥10 ∧ ¬tuple. Abbreviated terms with 351 graph-occurrence 1 (7 800) all reach ≥2 once the sequent is counted. 352 353 **Controlled probes (crafted theories, live server; see QUERIES.log):** 354 - `'12345678'` (10 chars, ×2) abbreviated; `'1234567'` (9, ×2) not ⇒ boundary = 10. 355 - `'ABCDEFGH'`/`'onceonly12'` (×1) not abbreviated; `'twicelong1'` (×2) is; 356 a 42-char constant (×1) not abbreviated ⇒ occ ≥ 2 is a hard gate at any length. 357 - `<'aa','bb','cc'>` (18 chars, ×2) not abbreviated while sibling `h('longarg123')` 358 (×2) is ⇒ tuple exclusion + bottom-up nesting. 359 360 **Residual (sufficiency):** for **AC/DH-operator** sub-terms (`^ * ⊕ ++ inv em 361 hp pmult`) tamarin counts occurrences over the *normalised* (AC-flattened, 362 DH-normal-form) term, not the surface rendering, so the same term can be 363 abbreviated in one proof state and not another (`em(hp($A),hp($B))` inline in 364 state `56eeb42d`, `EM1` in `54eb2e2c`). 93 % of apparent exceptions are such 365 AC/DH cases; the rest are validation artifacts from expanding shared 366 abbreviations before counting. Outside AC/DH terms the rule is exact. 367 368 The crate `abbrev::select` implements this rule (`MIN_ABBREV_LEN=10`, 369 `MIN_ABBREV_OCC=2`, tuple-excluded, bottom-up); tests reproduce every probe. 370 Byte-parity of the selection set is claimed for non-AC/DH terms and gated by the 371 caller supplying the full-system term multiset. 372 373 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 374 ## 6. System → graph mapping (§ Round-3 item 4) 375 376 From paired `main/proof` sequents and `interactive-graph-def` graphs, the 377 structural mapping a proof state yields (each point traces to §3d/§3f and the 378 content census; the *content* — which nodes/edges exist — is a solver GAP): 379 - a **rule instance** `#t : Rule[actions]` → a `record` node with groups 380 `{premises}|{info}|{conclusions}` (empty prem/concl dropped, info always kept); 381 - an **intruder-knowledge fact** → a gray `!KU( m ) @ #t` ellipse; 382 - a **protocol action/event** → a darkblue `Fact @ #t` ellipse; 383 - a **compressed intruder rule** → an uncolored `#t : rule` ellipse (§4 compression); 384 - an **unresolved node** referenced by an open premise → an `invtrapezium` 385 `(#var, idx)` (§3d); 386 - **edges** connect conclusion-port → premise-port (structural bold w10 [gray50]), 387 intruder deduction (red dashed), message (gray30), temporal order 388 (blue3/black dashed), etc. — the finite §3c vocabulary; 389 - ids from §3e; header from §4 (role); legend from §5. 390 391 `graph-clean::generate` implements this over an independent input model 392 (`System`/`GraphNode`/`RuleInstance`/`SysEdge`), taking node/edge lists, colors, 393 and cell text as INPUTS. `tests/generate_tests.rs` reproduces a live source-case 394 graph (`nsl_invtrap.dot`) **byte-exact** end-to-end. 395 396 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 397 ## 7. Simplification / abbreviation options (§ Round-3 item 5) 398 399 The UI's "Graph simplification off/L1/L2/L3" menu and abbreviation toggle are 400 cookie-driven and appended by the JS as graph-URL **query params** (mined from 401 `/static/js/tamarin-prover-ui.js`, confirmed by live diffs — QUERIES.log): 402 403 | param | UI sends when | observed effect | 404 |--------------------|----------------------------------|------------------------------------------| 405 | `simplification=N` | always (N=level, default 2) | **inert** — the *number* changes nothing (see below) | 406 | `uncompact=` | level 0 only | node COMPACTION off (lone intruder rule → full record vs ellipse) | 407 | `uncompress=` | level 0 only | system COMPRESSION off (Fresh sources + intruder rules re-expand to records) | 408 | `unabbreviate=` | abbreviate cookie off | abbreviation off (terms inlined, legend block omitted) | 409 | `no-auto-sources=` | auto-sources cookie off | disables auto-source precomputation | 410 | `clustering=true` | clustering cookie on | forces role clustering | 411 412 ### 7a. The L1/L2/L3 distinction — RESOLVED (channel-verified negative, Session 4) 413 414 The Round-3 GAP ("simplification 1/2/3 produced byte-identical graphs") is **not a 415 probing artifact and not a hidden session channel** — it is the genuine server 416 behavior: **there is no L1/L2/L3 distinction; the `simplification=N` *number* is 417 inert for every N.** Established under the session-state lens the follow-up 418 demanded: 419 420 - **Channel.** The served JS (`tamarin-prover-ui.js`, lines 78–100 / 476–500) 421 reads a *client-side* `simplification` cookie (default 2) and converts it into 422 the graph-URL query param; at level 0 it *additionally* appends `uncompact=` + 423 `uncompress=`. Levels 1/2/3 send **only** `simplification=N`. The server emits 424 **no `Set-Cookie` on any response** (root, case-graph, main/cases json, proof 425 method) — there is *no server-side session state*; the param is the only channel. 426 - **Change verified before diffing.** Driving the *flags* through the URL param 427 demonstrably changes the graph (e.g. rich NSLPK3 case `refined/6/5`: default 428 5 668 B → `uncompact=&uncompress=` 7 792 B), and the result is byte-identical 429 whether or not a matching cookie is also sent. So the URL param **is** honored. 430 - **The number is inert.** On that 17-node case graph and on a **39-node, 27 KB** 431 DH/bilinear proof-state graph (`eurosp19-eccDAA` analysed, 432 `proof/functional_correctness/_`), every probe of `simplification` ∈ 433 {0,1,2,3,4,5,10,99} — via URL param, via cookie, and via both together, and 434 crossed with every flag combination — collapses to a **single** md5. `flags_L0` 435 … `flags_L99` are byte-identical. (Second theory NAXOS_eCK case `refined/4/1` 436 reproduces this.) 437 - **Mechanism.** The UI's four-item menu (`for i<4`: off / L1 / L2 / L3) dresses up 438 a *binary*: "off" (level 0) = the UI adds the two flags; "L1/L2/L3" = no flags, 439 and the server never consumes the level integer. The real transforms are the 440 independent boolean flags, each with a distinct, measured effect on the 39-node 441 graph: `uncompact=` 39 nodes→richer records (27 361→30 880 B); `uncompress=` 442 39→**74** nodes (re-expands compressed chains, 31 407 B); `unabbreviate=` drops 443 the legend node (39→38) and inlines terms (36 690 B, 0 legend rows); 444 level 0 (`uncompact`+`uncompress`) = 74 nodes, byte-identical to the two flags 445 sent explicitly. 446 447 `graph-clean::options::Options` models the knobs and reproduces the exact query 448 string; `simplification=N` is carried but is documented inert. The abbreviation 449 on/off transform is applied directly (drop legend / inline). Remaining **GAP** 450 here: the *content* of compress/compact (a solver transform — the caller supplies 451 the already-(un)compressed node set). 452 453 -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 454 ## 8. What the crate reproduces vs. gaps 455 456 Reproduced & byte-tested against captured/live payloads: 457 - DOT document assembly: both headers, block/whitespace rule, node lines 458 (record/ellipse/plain/**invtrapezium**), edge lines with ports & attrs, cluster 459 subgraphs, legend sink-block + invis edges, empty graph. Round-trip reproduces 460 **12 022/12 022** corpus payloads byte-exact (`tests/roundtrip.rs`). 461 - **Node-id/port allocation** (§3e): `NodeIdAllocator`; validated 12 022/12 022 via 462 the crate model (`tests/alloc_corpus.rs`). 463 - **Record-cell rendering** (§3f): fact/function/tuple spacing, info-cell shape, 464 group-drop rule, record escaping, the wrap FORMAT (alignment indent + `\l`, 465 reproducing an observed wrapped cell byte-exact), and the wrap **DECISION** — 466 fixed width `FILL_WIDTH=87` with `fits_one_line` (top-level fit, boundary 467 byte-verified) and `paragraph_fill` (first-line packing) in `src/render.rs`. 468 - **System → graph GENERATION** (§6): `generate` over the independent `System` 469 model; reproduces a live source-case graph byte-exact (`tests/generate_tests.rs`). 470 - **Simplification/abbreviation options** (§7): `Options` query-string model; the 471 **L1/L2/L3 level number is proven inert** (§7a) — channel-verified negative. 472 - Abbreviation naming, numbering, legend HTML (65-space indent), and the SELECTION 473 rule (§5c, REPORT2.md), plus the cluster/compact trigger (§4). 474 475 Documented gaps (need the GPL solver or an unavailable backend): 476 - JSON graph backend format (unavailable / not in corpus). 477 - **Record-cell wrap residual** (§3f): the `fsep` one-element lookahead (±1 element 478 on continuation lines) and the exact boundary delimiter-peel; the width (87), 479 top-level fit, first-line packing and peel columns are pinned. 480 - **compress/compact content** (§4, §6): which nodes/edges a raw constraint system 481 yields — a solver transform. (The L1/L2/L3 level distinction is no longer a gap: 482 it is proven non-existent, §7a.) 483 - Per-rule/per-cluster **color hashes** (§3a, §4); the `trapezium` dual (§3d, unobserved). 484 - Abbreviation of AC/DH sub-terms (§5c residual, normalised-form occurrence). 485 - Canonical per-prefix numbering tie-break (§5b); empty-prefix start index. 486

## Session 4 — close L1/L2/L3 gap + re-attempt wrap DECISION (session-state lens)

### Channel investigation (served JS + response headers, all HTTP-observed OUTPUT)
- Fetched /static/js/tamarin-prover-ui.js from the black-box binary. The graph
  URL params are built from CLIENT-SIDE cookies (jquery-cookie): handleJson (lines
  78–100) appends `simplification=<cookie>` always, plus `uncompact=`+`uncompress=`
  ONLY when the cookie==0, plus `unabbreviate=`/`no-auto-sources=` per their
  cookies. Level-button loop is `for i<4` (off/L1/L2/L3), default cookie=2. No
  AJAX/POST sets a graph preference server-side; the POSTs are lemma editing only.
- `curl -i` on root, case-graph, main/cases json, and a proof-method request:
  **no Set-Cookie on ANY response** ⇒ no server-side session state; the URL query
  param is the sole channel. (This is what the follow-up's "session-state lens"
  warned to check; the channel is verified to be the URL param, not a cookie.)

### Live level probes (serve.sh wrapper, --port= form; ports 3200–3203)
- NSLPK3 :3200, richest source-case graph `refined/6/5` (17 nodes, 5 668 B). Matrix
  over `simplification` ∈ {0,1,2,3} × {URL param, cookie `-b`, both}: ALL byte-
  identical to baseline (one md5). Adding `uncompact=&uncompress=` (the real level-0
  flags) DID change it (7 792 B) and was identical with/without a cookie ⇒ URL param
  honored, change verified before diffing. Level number ∈ {0,1,2,3,4,5,10,99}
  crossed with 8 flag combos: within every combo, L1/L2/L3 collapse to 1 md5;
  `flags_L0`…`flags_L99` all one md5. Individual flag effects distinct (uncompact
  6 496, uncompress 6 326, unabbreviate 4 695, clustering 6 388 B).
- NAXOS_eCK :3201 case `refined/4/1` (2 912 B): L0=L1=L2=L3 identical, L0+flags
  differs. Reproduces the negative on a 2nd theory.
- eurosp19-eccDAA analysed :3203 (DH/bilinear, deep). Big proof-state graph
  `proof/functional_correctness/_` = **39 nodes, 27 361 B**. All level probes
  (url/cookie/both, {0,1,2,3,4,10}) → 1 md5; no Set-Cookie. Flag effects measured:
  uncompact 30 880 B (records richer, still 39), uncompress **74 nodes** 31 407 B,
  unabbreviate 38 nodes/36 690 B/0 legend rows, uncompact+uncompress==level0 (74
  nodes). ⇒ CONCLUSION: no L1/L2/L3 distinction; the level number is inert; "off"
  (L0) = the UI adding two flags. GAP closed (rigorous negative + mechanism).

### Wrap-DECISION probes (crafted single-node theories; autoprove + graph fetch)
- Route discovery: autoprove via GET /thy/trace/1/autoprove/idfs/0/False/proof/<L>
  (returns redirect to a new theory index); proof-state graph at
  interactive-graph-def/proof/<L>/_ . Confirmed the served JS appends graph params
  at line 922 (dotsrc + "?" + query_string) with no width/wrap parameter; layout
  east-size/west-size cookies are client-only (never sent) ⇒ wrap has NO channel;
  it is a fixed server-side pretty-printer, re-attacked by input control.
- Two sweeps solved live (ports 3220–3236, driver.py/gen_sweep.py):
  (a) `Out(<'a01',…,'aN'>)` N=1..29 — pure fill of 5-col elements;
  (b) `Out(<'aa…a'(P), 'y'>)` P=60..95 — one-column flat-width sweep.
  Findings: single-line iff flat width ≤ **87**; 88 breaks. First line packs 11
  elements + trailing ", " (ends col 83); continuation packs 12 (fsep lookahead).
  Closing `)` peels to col 0; tuple `>` peels to the `<` column; long unbreakable
  atoms overflow verbatim.
- Functor-length invariance: `Ba`/`Out`/`Fact12`/`Factabcdef` (len 2/3/6/10) — the
  flat-fit boundary is ALWAYS flat width 87 fits / 88 breaks ⇒ W=87 is ABSOLUTE
  from column 0, functor name included. Corpus cross-check: 99.2 % of 190 044
  physical lines have fact-content ≤ 87 (residual = unbreakable atoms > 87).
- Captured raw fixtures (fixtures/wrap_fixtures.json) for the crate tests. Servers
  stopped after probing. Implemented FILL_WIDTH=87 / fits_one_line / paragraph_fill
  in graph-clean/src/render.rs with tests reproducing these probes.

1	# Similarity audit — graphdot cluster (clean room vs GPL Haskell)
2	
3	Auditor role: SIMILARITY AUDITOR (may read both sides). Discipline:
4	abstraction–filtration–comparison. A match is a VIOLATION only if it is expression
5	that could not plausibly come from observing program OUTPUT: mirrored internal helper
6	decomposition where alternatives exist, matching internal (non-API, non-output) names,
7	comments echoing Haskell comments, algorithmic expression matching where the observed
8	behavior admits materially different implementations, or any content present in the
9	Haskell source but in NO observable output.
10	
11	## Sides compared
12	
13	CLEAN (reimplementation):
14	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/model.rs` — syntactic DOT graph model
15	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/dot.rs` — byte-exact DOT serializer
16	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/term.rs` — small term model for legend expansions
17	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/abbrev.rs` — prefix derivation, numbering, legend HTML
18	- `workspace/graph-clean/src/lib.rs` — module glue
19	
20	HASKELL (originals, GPL):
21	- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Dot.hs`
22	- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Graph/Abbreviation.hs`
23	- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Graph/GraphRepr.hs`
24	- `lib/theory/src/Theory/Constraint/System/Graph/Simplification.hs`
25	- (secondary dirty-room Rust ports: `crates/tamarin-server/src/graph/{repr,abbreviation,simplify,options}.rs`,
26	  `handlers/dot.rs` — translations of the Haskell; used only to understand the mapping)
27	
28	## Output-observability check (evidence the compatibility content is genuinely observed)
29	
30	Grepped the captured DOT payloads at `oracle/captured_responses`
31	(→ `tamarin-rs/scripts/.web_hs_cache`, 81 payloads). Confirmed present in OUTPUT:
32	- compact header incl. `packmode="cluster"` (8 payloads); simple header `nodesep="0.3"`.
33	- legend `<TABLE … CELLPADDING="1">` (73 payloads); `role=` on record nodes (79 payloads).
34	- abbrev names as PREFIX+counter: `AE1 AM1 … FS… EX…`; the exact 65-space hang indent
35	  before continuation `<TR>`; HTML escaping `&/</>`; exp rendering `'g'^…`.
36	- `FS` (from `F_status`) and `AM` (from `AMF_UE_NGAP_ID`) both appear as OUTPUT names.
37	
38	Consequence: every string constant and every naming/rendering behavior the clean side
39	reproduces is present in observed output, so it is compatibility content, not expression
40	copied from source.
41	
42	Grepped the clean side for distinctive Haskell internal identifiers
43	(`judgeTerm`, `getTermPrefix`, `abbreviateTerm`, `makeRecursive`, `prefixMap`, `allNames`,
44	`relativeOccs`, `termWeight`, `topoSort`, `roleColor`, `simpleHash`, `generateValue`,
45	`renderBalanced`, `scaleIndent`, `dotNodeCompact`, `mergeLessEdges`, `tryHideNode`,
46	`compressSystem`, `transitiveReduction`, …): NONE present.
47	
48	## VIOLATIONS
49	
50	None. No finding survives filtration as expression that could not have come from
51	observation. No redo instructions are issued.
52	
53	## Per-module analysis
54	
55	### model.rs vs GraphRepr.hs — CLEARED (different abstraction level)
56	The clean model is a *syntactic* DOT model: `Header{Simple,Compact}`,
57	`Stmt{Node,Edge,Cluster,RankBlock}`, `NodeKind{Record,Ellipse,Plain}`,
58	`Record{columns,fillcolor,fontcolor,role}`, `Cell{port,text}`, `Ellipse`, `EndPoint`,
59	`Cluster{label,color,body}`, `RankBlock{rank,body}`. The Haskell `GraphRepr` is a
60	*semantic* model: `Node{nNodeId,nNodeType}`, `NodeType{SystemNode ru, UnsolvedActionNode,
61	LastActionAtom, MissingNode}`, `Edge{SystemEdge,LessEdge,UnsolvedChain}`,
62	`Cluster{cName,cNodes,cEdges}`. The two carry different data (records/ellipses/attrs vs
63	rule instances / premise-conclusion indices). No shared internal names, no mirrored
64	decomposition. Fields present are dictated by what the DOT output contains. Not a violation.
65	
66	- `Role::UNDEFINED = "Undefined"` (model.rs:29) mirrors Haskell's
67	  `fromMaybe "Undefined" (getNodeRole node)` (Dot.hs:243) only because `role="Undefined"`
68	  is emitted into the DOT output (verified in 79 payloads). Sentinel derived from output.
69	- `infer_header` = "compact iff any node has a non-Undefined role" (model.rs:56-64).
70	  Haskell's actual trigger is `null grClusters` (Dot.hs:503). These are different internal
71	  predicates that happen to coincide behaviorally (clusters are built only from role-bearing
72	  nodes). The clean side chose a *different* expression of the same observable correlation.
73	  Not a violation.
74	
75	### dot.rs vs Dot.hs — CLEARED (output strings + independent structure)
76	- `header_lines` Simple/Compact attribute lists (dot.rs:38-69) are identical in content

152	traces to observable OUTPUT (exact DOT/HTML strings, abbrev names, surface-syntax renderings)
153	or to behavior that the output forces. No mirrored internal decomposition, no shared internal
154	names, no echoed comments, and none of the source-only machinery (weight/greedy abbreviation,
155	collision avoidance, topo-sort ordering, color hashing, system simplification/clustering)

158	## Round 2 incremental audit
159	
160	Scope: the abbreviation SELECTION policy added this round —
161	`abbrev::select` / `select_with` / `MIN_ABBREV_LEN=10` / `MIN_ABBREV_OCC=2` and the
162	tuple-exclusion + bottom-up-ordering it uses (`abbrev.rs:157-210`), the supporting
163	`term.rs` additions (`render_len`, `is_tuple`, `for_each_subterm`, `size`,
164	`term.rs:91-134`), and the `select_*` tests (`tests/abbrev_tests.rs:238-320`).
165	Haskell original: the term-judging / selection machinery in
166	`Graph/Abbreviation.hs` — `judgeTerm` (83-101), `computeAbbreviations`/`go`
167	(166-231), `getFactTerms`/`getSubTerms` (209-215), `defaultAbbreviationOptions`
168	(66-72). Key question posed: does the clean rule reproduce the Haskell EXPRESSION
169	(constants, weight formula, decomposition) or only observable BEHAVIOR?
170	
171	### Side-by-side (abstraction–filtration)
172	
173	| aspect | Haskell (Abbreviation.hs) | Clean (`select`) | observable? | disposition |
174	|--------|---------------------------|------------------|-------------|-------------|
175	| length gate ≥10 | `judgeTerm … \| termWeight < 10 = -1` where `termWeight = length $ render $ prettyLNTerm replacedTerm` | `render_len() >= MIN_ABBREV_LEN` (10) | YES — corpus floor is exactly 10 over 97 538 legends, and probe `'12345678'`(10)→abbrev vs `'1234567'`(9)→not | FILTERED (output-forced boundary) |
176	| occurrence gate ≥2 | `\| relativeOccs <= 1 = -1` | count over `roots` `>= MIN_ABBREV_OCC` (2) | YES — probe: 42-char×1 not abbreviated, 12-char×2 abbreviated | FILTERED |
177	| tuple/pair exclusion | `getFactTerms = filter (not . isPair) …` | skip `t.is_tuple()` | YES — 0/97 538 legends is a top-level tuple; probe left an 18-char tuple×2 inline | FILTERED |
178	| length measured on the **rendered string** | `length $ render $ prettyLNTerm` (of the abbrev-**applied** term) | `render_full().chars().count()` (fully **expanded**, no abbrev applied) | YES (measurable per legend) — and the two measures *differ* in the iterative case | FILTERED / DIVERGENT |
179	| product weight `relativeOccs * termWeight` | present (the ranking value) | **absent** | NO — a non-observable internal ordering weight | NOT reproduced — positive signal |
180	| greedy max-weight pick + per-subterm occurrence decrement loop (`go`) | present | **absent** (flat filter, no iteration) | NO | NOT reproduced |
181	| soft-limit `aoAbbrevsSoftLimit=10` / `aoAlwaysAbbrevWeight=30` cutoff | present (178) | **absent** | NO — source-only constants | NOT reproduced — positive signal |
182	| `relativeOccs` legend-only special case (`occs==1 && legendOccs==[1] → 0`) | present (97-101) | **absent** | NO | NOT reproduced |
183	| subterm enumeration | one level: `getSubTerms t = t : ts` (215) | **fully recursive** pre-order `for_each_subterm` | — | DIVERGENT (opposite depth), not mirrored |
184	| ordering of survivors | decreasing weight (`sortOn Down`, greedy-first) | **ascending** `render_len` (shortest/inner first) | numbering only partly observable | OPPOSITE direction — not mirrored |
185	| occurrence universe | graph `grNodes`/cluster nodes (192-205) | whole constraint system (`roots` = node-fact args **and** sequent terms), derived because legends carry terms drawn nowhere | observation-derived | DIVERGENT, not mirrored |
186	
187	### Assessment of the key question
188	The clean rule reproduces the observable BEHAVIOR, not the Haskell EXPRESSION.
189	The three gates it keeps (len≥10, occ≥2, not-tuple) are each independently
190	witnessed by corpus statistics and by controlled boundary probes
191	(QUERIES.log / BEHAVIOR §5c), so reproducing them — including the two literal
192	constants 10 and 2, which the corpus pins directly — is compatibility content.
193	The predicate "len≥10 AND occ≥2 AND not-tuple" is the minimal expression of that
194	observed selection behavior and admits no materially different implementation of
195	the predicate itself; it is output-forced, not copied.
196	
197	Decisively, the entire non-observable apparatus that surrounds those gates in the
198	source is ABSENT on the clean side: the product weight formula, the greedy
199	max-weight/decrement iteration, the soft-limit + always-abbrev-weight cutoff
200	(constants 10/30), the `relativeOccs` legend special-case, and the one-level
201	`getSubTerms`. Where the clean side had latitude it chose *differently* from the
202	source — full-recursive enumeration vs one-level, ascending-length ordering vs
203	greedy decreasing-weight, fully-expanded length vs abbrev-applied length, a
204	whole-system occurrence universe vs `grNodes`. These divergences are affirmative
205	evidence of derivation from observation rather than from the source.
206	
207	No shared internal names (clean `select`/`select_with`/`render_len`/`is_tuple`/
208	`for_each_subterm`/`size` vs HS `judgeTerm`/`computeAbbreviations`/`termWeight`/
209	`relativeOccs`/`getSubTerms`/`getFactTerms`/`aoAbbrevsSoftLimit`). No echoed
210	comments — the clean comments cite the clean room's own corpus/probe evidence,
211	whereas the HS comments describe the weight design and its `cleandot.py` origin.
212	
213	### VIOLATIONS (Round 2)
214	None. No finding survives filtration as non-observable expression. No redo
215	instructions. VERDICT: PASS.

226	EXPRESSION materially different or output-forced, given the corpus-derived

235	- Clean allocator (alloc.rs:19–55): one `usize` counter; `record(n_cells)` takes

239	  map (map (uncurry D.portField))` (Dot.hs:310–312) over `filter (not . null)

249	
250	- Fact spacing `Name( a, b )` / `Name( )` (`pad`, render.rs:77–83), metachar escaping

287	- Edge-style vocabulary (`EdgeStyle`, generate.rs:114–151): a flat enum whose eight

293	  grouped by observed bytes, not by the Haskell's source-structural taxonomy. The 11
294	  fixed styles are the observed census (mine_content.py). Output-forced strings +
295	  divergent classification → not a violation.

297	### options.rs vs Graph.hs `GraphOptions` / `SimplificationLevel` — CLEARED
298	
299	- Clean `Options {level, abbreviate, compact, compress, clustering}` (options.rs:40–52,
300	  default level 2 / abbreviate / compact / compress / no-cluster) vs Haskell
301	  `GraphOptions {_goSimplificationLevel(SL2), _goShowAutoSource(False), _goClustering,
302	  _goAbbreviate(True), _goCompress(True)}` (Graph.hs:56–73). The overlapping fields and

8	--
9	-- System simplification functions that were originally contained in the Dot module

11	module Theory.Constraint.System.Graph.Simplification (
12	      simplifySystem
13	    , compressSystem
14	  )
15	  where
16	
17	import qualified Data.Map                 as M
18	import           Data.Maybe
19	import qualified Data.Set                 as S
20	import           Data.List                (foldl')
21	import           Control.Basics
22	import           Extension.Data.Label
23	-- import           Theory.Constraint.System
24	import           Theory                   
25	import qualified Data.DAG.Simple          as Dag
26	import           Data.Monoid              (Any(..))
27	
28	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
29	-- Compressed versions of a sequent (originally from Dot module)
30	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
31	
32	-- | Drop 'Less' atoms entailed by the edges of the 'System'.
33	dropEntailedOrdConstraints :: System -> System
34	dropEntailedOrdConstraints se =
35	    modify sLessAtoms (S.filter (not . entailed)) se
36	  where
37	    edges               = rawEdgeRel se
38	    entailed (LessAtom from to _) = to `S.member` Dag.reachableSet [from] edges
39	
40	-- | Unsound compression of the sequent that drops fully connected learns and
41	-- knows nodes.
42	compressSystem :: System -> System
43	compressSystem se0 =
44	    foldl' (flip tryHideNodeId) se (frees (get sLessAtoms se, get sNodes se))
45	  where
46	    se = dropEntailedOrdConstraints se0
47	
48	-- | Simplify the system up to the sent level, 
49	-- | for level 3, we will simplify the lesses of system by transitive reduction
50	-- | for level 2, we will apply the transitive reduction but keep the 
51	-- | formula constraint 
52	-- | for level 1, there's no transitive reduction applied
53	simplifySystem :: Int -> System -> System
54	simplifySystem i sys
55	    | i==2 = transitiveReduction sys False
56	    | i==3 = transitiveReduction sys True
57	    | otherwise = sys
58	
59	-- | Simplify the system by transitive reduction (constraint of formula won't  
60	-- | be applied if totalRed is False) but not for a system which has a graph cyclic
61	transitiveReduction :: System -> Bool -> System
62	transitiveReduction sys totalRed=
63	    if Dag.cyclic oldLesses
64	        then sys
65	        else   modify sLessAtoms
66	            ( S.intersection ( S.fromList newLesses) ) sys
67	    where
68	        oldLessesWithR = S.toList $ get sLessAtoms sys
69	        oldLesses = rawLessRel sys
70	        newLesses = if totalRed
71	            then [ la | la@(LessAtom x y _) <- oldLessesWithR,
72	                            (x,y) `elem` Dag.transRed oldLesses ]
73	            else [ la | la@(LessAtom x y z) <- oldLessesWithR,
74	                            (x,y) `elem` Dag.transRed oldLesses || z == Formula || z == Adversary ]
75	
76	
77	-- | @hideTransferNode v se@ hides node @v@ in sequent @se@ if it is a
78	-- transfer node; i.e., a node annotated with a rule that is one of the
79	-- special intruder rules or a rule with with at most one premise and
80	-- at most one conclusion and both premises and conclusions have incoming
81	-- respectively outgoing edges.
82	--
83	-- The compression is chosen such that unly uninteresting nodes are that have
84	-- no open goal are suppressed.
85	tryHideNodeId :: NodeId -> System -> System
86	tryHideNodeId v se = fromMaybe se $ do
87	    guard $  (lvarSort v == LSortNode)
88	          && notOccursIn unsolvedChains
89	          && notOccursIn (get sFormulas)
